title: Jean-Christophe Pariisissa I: V. Markkinatori
summaries: "Jean-Christophe Pariisissa I" by Romain Rolland is a novel published serially from 1904 to 1912. It follows the life of Jean-Christophe Krafft, a German musical genius, from birth through his journey toward wisdom and harmony. The child prodigy faces trials that test his passions and character as he grows from a young pianist performing for royalty to a struggling composer in Paris. This work, which embodied hopes for Franco-German reconciliation, won Rolland prestigious literary recognition and contributed to his Nobel Prize in Literature.
